zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Bertram Baron of Bothal於1193年出生在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德時，他的父親Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal34歲，母親Mabel de Clare32歲。他於1218年與Mrs Sarah Bertram在英國諾圖波藍德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1238年在他的家鄉享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Bertram Baron of Bothal于1193年出生在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德时，他的父亲Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal34岁，母亲Mabel de Clare32岁。他于1218年与Mrs Sarah Bertram在英国諾圖波藍德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1238年在他的家乡享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bertram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English (mainly in southeastern Scotland, of Norman origin), French, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertram, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. The raven was the bird of Odin, king of the gods, in ancient Germanic mythology. The personal name was common in France throughout the Middle Ages (especially in the form Bertrand ), where its popularity was increased by the fame of the troubadour Bertrand de Born (c. 1140–1214). The name was taken to England by the Normans in the forms Bertran(d), Bertram, and Bartram.

variants.name: Beltrami
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Dino, Marco, Pio, Sal, Tullio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Beltrame, from an ancient Germanic name composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Bartram
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: variant of   Bertram  .

History: William Bartram, a Quaker, had a large farm near Darby, PA, when his eldest son, John, the first American botanist, was born in 1699. John conducted botanical experiments at his own farm in Kingsessing, PA, near Philadelphia.

variants.name: Bertman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Frieda, Wilhelm.
Americanized form of German Bertmann: altered form (by folk etymology) of Bertram .

variants.name: Bethke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Eldor, Ewald, Otto, Rudi, Wilhelmina.
North German: from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal names   Bertram   or   Berthold  . See also   Beth  .

variants.name: Bertrand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Monique, Francois, Gilles, Raoul, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Odette.

French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Bertrand, a variant of Bertram , coined by folk etymology under the influence of the present participle endings -and, -ant.
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Beltran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Pedro, Jorge, Mario, Ricardo, Francisco, Rafael.
Spanish (Beltrán), Catalan, and southern French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Beltramo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo.
Italian: from the medieval personal name Beltramo, of ancient Germanic origin (see Bertram ).

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

variants.name: Battermann
meanings: German:
possibly an occupational name for a merchant or peddler, from Bartermann.
alternatively, perhaps from a short form of the personal name Bertram + Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bertran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jorge, Eduardo, Enrique, Joaquin, Ricardo, Aleida, Ana, Ana Maria, Elena, Felipe, Jeronimo.  French Andre.
French, Occitan, and Catalan: variant of   Bertram   or   Bertrand  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Bertram Baron of Bothal was born in 1193, in Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal, was 34 and his mother, Mabel de Clare, was 32. He married Mrs Sarah Bertram in 1218, in Northumberland, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died about 1238, in his hometown, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Bertram Baron of Bothal est né en 1193, à Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Mabel de Clare, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Mrs Sarah Bertram en 1218, à Northumberland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé environ 1238, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Bertram Baron of Bothal 1193 in Northumberland, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal 34 und seine Mutter Mabel de Clare 32. Er hat 1218 in Northumberland, England Mrs Sarah Bertram geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb um 1238 im Alter von 46 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Bertram Baron of Bothal è nato il nel 1193 nel Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Mabel de Clare, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Mrs Sarah Bertram nel 1218, nel Northumberland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì circa 1238, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 46 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Bertram Baron of Bothal(이)가 1193년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Mabel de Clare(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1218년 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 Mrs Sarah Bertram(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1238년 그의 고향에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Bertram Baron of Bothal nasceu em 1193, em Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Mabel de Clare, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs Sarah Bertram em 1218, em Northumberland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1238, em sua cidade natal, com 46 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Bertram Baron of Bothal родился в 1193 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Mabel de Clare, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Sarah Bertram в 1218 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер около 1238 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 46 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Bertram Baron of Bothal nació en 1193, en Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Mabel de Clare, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Mrs Sarah Bertram en 1218, en Northumberland, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1238, en su pueblo, a la edad de 46 años.
